  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Double-tapped circuit breakers that are not listed or designed for terminating more than one conductor."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the extra conductors from the non-listed breakers.",
    "Install additional single-pole breakers if space permits, or use pigtails to combine the conductors under a single wire connector before terminating to the breaker."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Section 110.14(A) states that terminals for more than one conductor shall be so identified. Standard circuit breakers are typically only listed for a single conductor unless explicitly marked otherwise by the manufacturer. Double-tapping non-listed breakers creates a fire hazard due to loose connections and overheating.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14(A)",
    "NEC Art. 408"
  ]
}
